title: Polymnia africana
intro: Polymnia africana, also known as African polynnia, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, traditionally used in African folk medicine. The health benefits of Polymnia africana include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Polymnia africana is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and topical creams for wound healing and pain relief.
taxonomy: Polymnia africana is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family, such as flower structure and leaf morphology.
